behavior_summary: U8P_LEGLEFT::use is a process triggered when the body-part object is used; it spawns the BODYPART::func0270 routine for this instance to run the part's 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' behavior (animation/effect/process).

U8P_LEGLEFT::hit(uword, word) handles being struck: it calls FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) to clear an associated reference, then checks Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this) and, if zero, spawns BODYPART::func0431 for this instance to run the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit-reaction routine. The getNumProcesses call acts as a guard to avoid duplicate U8P_LEGLEFT::hit processes.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LEGLEFT::use: single-step spawn of BODYPART::func0270 for this instance (no branching).
U8P_LEGLEFT::hit: immediate cleanup/reset via FREE::resetRef, followed by a conditional branch based on Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this); spawn BODYPART::func0431 only when that count is zero.
open_questions: What exact gameplay effects/animations are implemented inside BODYPART::func0270 and BODYPART::func0431 (spawned routines) — current excerpt spawns them but does not reveal their contents.
What semantic meaning do the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit parameters 'uword' and 'word' carry (damage amount, attacker id, U8P_LEGLEFT::hit type, etc.)?
What precisely is process type 0x020A (the type counted by Kernel::getNumProcesses) in this game's process taxonomy, and why is it used as the guard for spawning the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit routine?

main_function.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::use
main_function.purpose: When the player uses/activates this body-part object, start the part's 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' routine by spawning BODYPART::func0270 for this instance.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as a process of type 0x0207 when the object is 'used' by the player/Avatar.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' interaction by spawning the part's U8P_LEGLEFT::use routine.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::hit
function_map.role: Handle U8P_LEGLEFT::hit/damage events: reset references and conditionally spawn the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit-reaction routine.

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0270
function_map.role: Spawned by U8P_LEGLEFT::use() to perform the 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' behavior for the body part (animation/effect/process).

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0431
function_map.role: Spawned by U8P_LEGLEFT::hit() to perform the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit/damage reaction behavior for the body part.

external_calls.purpose: Start the body-part's 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' routine for this instance (spawn).
Clear/reset a reference/registration id 0x0202 associated with this instance as immediate housekeeping after a U8P_LEGLEFT::hit.
Query number of running processes of type 0x020A on this instance to decide whether to spawn the U8P_LEGLEFT::hit routine (serves as a guard against duplicates).
Start the body-part's U8P_LEGLEFT::hit-reaction routine for this instance when no existing 0x020A processes are present (spawn).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LEGLEFT::use is a process triggered when the body-part object is used; it spawns the BODYPART::func0270 routine for this instance to run the part's 'U8P_LEGLEFT::use' behavior (animation/effect/process).
